zh.lifeSummary: 當Wilhelm II. von Berg於大約1348年出生在爾利時，他的父親Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg28歲，母親Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg35歲。他於1360年與Anna von Wittelsbach結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1408年6月25日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪瑟爾多福享年61歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Wilhelm II. von Berg于大约1348年出生在爾利时，他的父亲Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg28岁，母亲Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg35岁。他于1360年与Anna von Wittelsbach结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1408年6月25日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪瑟爾多福享年61岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Wilhelm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from wil ‘will’ + helm ‘helmet’; an ancient royal name. See also William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

en.lifeSummary: When Wilhelm II. von Berg was born about 1348, in Jülich, his father, Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg, was 28 and his mother, Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, was 35. He married Anna von Wittelsbach in 1360. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 25 June 1408, in Düsseldorf, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 61, and was buried in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wilhelm II. von Berg est né environ 1348, à Jülich, son père, Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Anna von Wittelsbach en 1360. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 25 juin 1408, à Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wilhelm II. von Berg um 1348 in Jülich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg 28 und seine Mutter Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg 35. Er hat 1360 Anna von Wittelsbach geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 25. Juni 1408 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wilhelm II. von Berg è nato il circa 1348 nel Jülich, il suo padre, Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Anna von Wittelsbach nel 1360. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 25 giugno 1408, nel Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Wilhelm II. von Bergが約1348年にジuリチで生まれた時，父Gerhard I. von Jülich-Bergは28歳，母Margarete von Ravensberg-Bergは35歳であった。1360年にAnna von Wittelsbachと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1408年6月25日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンドuセルドルフで61歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンルヘイニスチ-バージッシャー クレイスオデンサルアルテンバーグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Wilhelm II. von Berg(이)가 대략 1348년 ju리치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1360년 Anna von Wittelsbach(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1408년 6월 25일 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 드u셀도르프에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔 알텐버그에 묻혔다.

pt.Ravensberg, Bad Doberan, Mecklenburg-Vor: Ravensberg, Kreis Bad Doberan, Rostock, Deutsche Demokratische Republik
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wilhelm II. von Berg nasceu aproximadamente 1348, em Jülich, seu pai, Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna von Wittelsbach em 1360. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 25 de junho de 1408, em Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.Odenthal-Altenberg Rheinisch-Bergischer: алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия
ru.Ravensberg, Bad Doberan, Mecklenburg-Vor: равенсбирг, крис бад добиран, ростокк, Восточная Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wilhelm II. von Berg родился около 1348 г. в местности джuлич, его отцу по имени Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna von Wittelsbach в 1360 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 25 июня 1408 г. в местности дuсселдорф, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Wilhelm II. von Berg nació aproximadamente 1348, en Jülich, su papá, Gerhard I. von Jülich-Berg, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margarete von Ravensberg-Berg, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Anna von Wittelsbach en 1360. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 25 de junio de 1408, en Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
